titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/291

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=266 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:43:41.174899+00 | translation moa=267 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:43:44.574843+00

p.cl: Ricardus dei gratia Dunkeldensis Episcopus  vniuersis sancte matris Ecclesie filiis Salutem .

Sciant presentes et futuri . Nos concessisse et hac carta
nostra confirmasse . deo et Ecclesie sancti Iohannis apostoli de Incheaffran et fratribus ibidem deo seruientibus et seruiturtis terram nostram Im mader
nin . que terra uocatur abthen . per suas rectas diuisas . cum omnibus Iustis pertinenciis suis . ad peticionem Comitis . Gileberti de strath
erne  et fratris eius . M . in redemptionem anime \nostre/ et antecessorum nostrorum et predicti Comitis . G . et suorum . Tenendam predictis fratribus et eorum suc
cessoribus In liberam et perpetuam Elemosinam reddendo nobis et successoribus nostris annuatim unam marcam argenti . scilicet Dimidiam
ad Pentecosten . et Dimidiam ad festum sancti Martini . Volumus itaque et precipimus ut hiidem fratres predicti et eorum successores predictam terram
teneant et possideant de nobis et successoribus nostris . ita libere et quiete plenarie et honorifice ab omni exactione et consuetudine et ser
uitio seculari ad nos pertinente . In boscho . et plano . In pratis et pascuis . In aquis et molendinis . et omnibus aliis aisiamentis ad
eandem terram pertinentibus . sicut aliqua terra in Episcopatu nostro liberius plenius et honorificentius tenetur et possidetur . et sicut carta Iohannis Episcopi
predecessoris nostri testatur . salua tamen predicta pensione .

Hiis Testibus . Henrico archidiacono nostro . Radulfo . Berndardo . et Thoma
Capellanis nostris . Ludouico et Iohanne clericis nostris . Bricio persona de chref . Roberto capellano de Tubermora . Gileberto filio archidiaconi de Strahern .
Richard, by the grace of God Bishop of Dunkeld, to all sons of Holy Mother Church, Greeting:

Let present and future know that we have granted, and by this our charter made firm, to God and to the church of St John the Apostle at Inchaffray, and to the brethren serving God there now and in the future, our land in Madderty, the land that is called ‘apdaine’, by its correct bounds, with all is just pertinents, at the request of Earl Gilbert of Strathearn and his brother Malise, for the redemption of our soul and of our ancestors’, and of the the foresaid Earl Gilbert and his own people. For the foresaid brethren and their successors to hold in free and perpetual alms, by rendering to us and our successors annually one merk of silver, that is, half at Pentecost and half at the feast of St Martin. We therefore will and enjoin that the same foresaid brethren and their successors hold and possess the foresaid land of us and our successors, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, from every exaction and custom and secular service pertaining to us, in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, in waters and mills, and all other easements pertaining to the same land, just as any land in our bishopric is freely and honourably held and possessed, and just as the charter of Bishop John, our predecessor, bears witness, but saving the foresaid pension.

With these witnesses, Henry our archdeacon, Ralph, Bernard, and Thomas, our chaplains, Louis and John our clerks, Brice the parson of Crieff, Robert the chaplain of Tibbermore, Gilbert the son of the archdeacon of Strathearn.
